rationale: Melumad et al. (2019) is used in the citing paper's core framing, where the authors position their work as expanding prior evidence that external factors can shift review emotional content. The citation is also used in study design and measurement contexts (e.g., within-writer logic to reduce selection concerns and human-judgment coding of positive vs. negative emotion), indicating more than a passing mention. The citing paper does not dispute the smartphone findings; it adds a new antecedent (incentives) to the same broad question of what shapes review language.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Melumad et al.'s finding that device context affects emotional expression in UGC and extends that external-cue line by testing incentives as another causal driver of review valence. It also borrows related methodological framing and coding practices when operationalizing review positivity.
